howItWorks.intro: This work-life balance calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is good work-life balance?
faq.answer: There's no universal standard, but key indicators include: working 40-45 hours/week, exercising 3+ times/week, getting 7+ hours sleep, having meaningful social connections, and using your vacation time. The right balance is personal.

faq.question: How many hours is too many to work?
faq.answer: Research shows productivity drops significantly after 50 hours/week and collapses after 55. The WHO found working 55+ hours/week increases stroke risk by 35% and heart disease by 17%. Sustainable high performance requires recovery.

faq.question: How can I improve work-life balance?
faq.answer: Set firm boundaries (no email after 7pm), schedule personal time like meetings, take your vacation days, learn to say no, batch similar tasks, delegate, and remember that being busy isn't the same as being productive.

faq.question: Is the Work-Life Balance Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard mental health and wellness formulas trusted by individuals seeking balance. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Work-Life Balance Calculator?
sections.body: The Work-Life Balance Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals seeking balance who need quick, accurate calculations in the mental health and wellness space. By entering your weekly work hours, exercise days/week, social/hobby hours/week, you get instant results including balance score, grade, top area to improve. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting balance score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In mental health and wellness, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals seeking balance worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to support your mental well-being with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weekly work hours and need to find the right balance score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weekly Work Hours
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your weekly work hours — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: exercise days/week, social/hobby hours/week, sleep hours/night, vacation days used/year. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your balance score, grade, top area to improve. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weekly work hours before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both balance score and grade — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to support your mental well-being.
If you're unsure about your vacation days used/year, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
